_comment: Template/example for architect draft requirement JSON. Used by the requirement-architect agent. Template path: requirements/requirement-architect/templates/requirement-architect-draft.schema.json. Save to: requirements/architect/YYYY-MM-DD-{slug}.requirements-draft.json
title: Lab Booking System

readiness.notes: Suitable for first-cut BA review, with several open questions remaining before detailed decomposition.

executive_summary: The institution needs a clearer, more consistent way for staff and students to discover equipment availability and request bookings, while giving operational stakeholders better oversight and approval handling for restricted equipment.
problem_statement: Academic staff and students cannot easily discover which laboratory equipment is available or book it in advance, leading to scheduling conflicts and underutilisation of shared resources.
business_objectives: Improve visibility of equipment availability before requests are made.
Reduce booking conflicts and manual coordination effort.
Introduce a clearer business process for restricted equipment approvals.
Provide more consistent notifications and oversight for stakeholders.

stakeholders.involvement: Books equipment for coursework or research.
Books equipment and may act in approval-related processes.
Oversees business rules, availability, and operational readiness.
Provides oversight and may review utilisation outcomes or policy impacts.

in_scope: Viewing equipment availability by date and time
Supporting approval handling for restricted equipment
Providing notifications for booking status changes
out_of_scope: Procurement or inventory management of equipment

current_state_overview: Availability information is not consistently visible before users make requests.
Coordination depends heavily on manual communication and operational knowledge.
pain_points: Operational teams spend time resolving clashes manually.
Restricted equipment requests are not handled through a consistent business process.
target_future_state: Users can follow a clearer self-service process for availability checking and booking requests.
Restricted equipment requests follow a visible and consistent approval path.
Stakeholders receive better notifications and high-level oversight of booking activity.

capability_themes.name: Availability visibility
capability_themes.intent: Allow users to understand what equipment is available before making a request.
capability_themes.why_it_matters: Reduces uncertainty and avoids unnecessary conflicts.
included_in_theme: Viewing availability by date and time
Seeing restrictions or availability conditions at a high level
open_points: [Open] How should temporary maintenance-related unavailability be communicated to users?

capability_themes.name: Booking request management
capability_themes.intent: Support a more consistent way for users to request, amend, and cancel bookings.
capability_themes.why_it_matters: Improves user experience and reduces manual coordination effort.
open_points: [Open] Are there policy limits on amendment or cancellation timing?

capability_themes.name: Approval handling and oversight
capability_themes.intent: Ensure restricted requests are reviewed consistently and stakeholders have appropriate visibility.
capability_themes.why_it_matters: Supports governance, safety, and operational control.
open_points: [Open] What level of reporting is needed in the initial release?

business_rules_constraints: A booking must not overlap with an existing confirmed booking for the same equipment.
Restricted equipment requests must be reviewed before they are confirmed.
[Assumed] Requests should normally be submitted at least 24 hours before the requested booking start time.
dependencies_touchpoints: Lab Administration for availability rules and operational ownership
Academic Staff or equivalent approvers for restricted requests
[Assumed] Existing institutional authentication and email services
risks_considerations: Policy details for cancellations and recurring bookings are not yet agreed.
Operational ownership for maintenance-related availability updates may need clarification.
Reporting expectations may expand unless scope is kept explicit in the draft review.

assumptions.statement: Existing institutional services can be reused where appropriate.
Single sign-on can be reused for the future process.
Institutional email services can support notifications.

open_questions.question: Should bookings be cancellable after the start time?
Is there a maximum booking duration per equipment type?
What reporting scope is required in the first cut?
